eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue incremental assaults toward Vasylivka in Donetsk Oblast's Pokrovsk direction, targeting adjacent areas like Novooleksandrivka and Bilytske, but have not entered the settlement as of early May 2026. Ukrainian troops recently rolled back Russian gains made earlier this year on the Vasylivka front, securing supply lines to Stepnohirsk, intensifying fighting in Prymorske and Plavni, and pressuring a Russian salient east of the main line amid drone strikes on logistics routes. This attritional frontline reflects Russia's manpower push against Ukraine's fortified defenses, with no major breakthroughs in the past 30 days; ongoing ground clashes and potential escalation from missile barrages could shift control before any resolution date.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

events.title: SpaceX IPO Closing Market Cap (Lower Strikes)
events.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders assign a 60.5% implied probability to SpaceX closing its IPO above $2 trillion market cap, driven by the company's April 2026 S-1 filing signaling a $1.75–2 trillion target ahead of an early June roadshow—the largest public offering in history. Starlink's expansion to 10 million subscribers, generating $7.2 billion in adjusted EBITDA (63% margins) from satellite broadband, now dominates revenue alongside reusable Falcon 9 dominance (165 launches in 2025). Recent Starship flight showcases and direct-to-cell spectrum deals amplify growth narratives, elevating sentiment beyond February's $1.25 trillion secondary valuation. Lower brackets trail as consensus anticipates premium pricing, with full S-1 disclosure late May as the key catalyst amid FAA approvals and execution risks.

markets.question: Will Denman Rooke win the 2026 Galway West by-election?
markets.description:
A by-election for a seat from the Galway West constituency in the Dáil Éireann, the lower house of the Irish parliament, is expected to take place sometime in 2026 to fill the vacancy left by the election of Catherine Connoly as President of Ireland.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Galway West seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Irish Times/TG4/Ipsos B&A poll from May 1-5 captures trader sentiment in the tight Galway West by-election race, showing Fine Gael Senator Seán Kyne leading Independent Ireland's Noel Thomas 17%-16% on first preferences, with Labour's Helen Ogbu third at 12% among 17 candidates. Under single transferable vote, fragmented support makes transfers pivotal, with traders pricing Thomas the slight favorite at implied 52% win probability over Kyne's 44%, likely anticipating anti-government momentum and flows from independents like Mike Cubbard amid historical by-election swings against incumbents. Cost of living—groceries, housing shortages, infrastructure delays—dominates canvassing; upcoming RTÉ debates and youth turnout could separate frontrunners before May 22 polling.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 2026 parliamentary elections, where no party secured a majority in Slovenia's 90-seat National Assembly, outgoing Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement failed to form a coalition by April 20 and shifted to opposition. President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to nominate a prime minister-designate, tasking lawmakers with proposing a candidate able to build a majority. Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS), with allied center-right parties like New Slovenia (led by Jernej Vrtovec), has advanced coalition guidelines as of early May, positioning Janša as the frontrunner with trader consensus at 92% implied probability. Final agreement and a confidence vote remain pending, though discord over policy details or holdouts could delay or derail confirmation.

If no 2026 Michigan Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Michigan Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Perry Johnson leads trader consensus at 55.5% implied probability to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, driven by his self-funding advantage—including a $4.7 million media reservation—and recent confirmation of sufficient valid petition signatures for ballot access, mitigating risks from his 2022 disqualification. U.S. Rep. John James trails at 38%, buoyed by name recognition from prior Senate bids and leads in April polls like Glengariff (37% vs. Johnson's 20%), but markets discount this amid Johnson's ad blitz and voter enthusiasm noted in prediction platforms. Dueling signature challenges filed this week by each camp's supporters add uncertainty, while Tom Leonard's April dropout narrows the field, with Anthony Hudson at 4% as a distant third; upcoming validation hearings and debates could shift dynamics in this closely contested race.

markets.question: Will NYC Mayor post 120-139 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani (@NYCMayor), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani's (@NYCMayor) consistent posting rhythm of 3-5 updates daily since his January 2026 inauguration as New York City mayor underpins the trader consensus favoring 20-39 posts from May 5-12, with early-week activity—including three posts on May 7 highlighting the 5 Boro Bike Tour completion and Teacher Appreciation Week—aligning traders' projections for the full eight-day span. This pattern echoes resolutions of prior weekly markets in the same range, reflecting his focus on substantive policy announcements like immigration advocacy and public safety briefings rather than high-volume engagement. A commanding 97% implied probability persists amid steady habits, though an unforeseen crisis such as a citywide emergency, major legislative vote, or high-profile diplomatic event could spur a posting surge into higher brackets before May 12 resolution.

Recent developments, including Zelenskyy’s May 1 response to Putin’s Victory Day ceasefire proposal on May 9 and announcements of expanded Ukrainian strikes like sea drone hits on Russian tankers, signal potential posting spikes from daily addresses and real-time updates during escalation or diplomacy. Historical rates of 8-12 posts per day in active weeks keep higher bins viable, but lulls from troop reforms or negotiations could suppress volume. Victory Day events and any truce signals through May 15 will likely determine separation, with military actions favoring 60+ outcomes and de-escalation tilting lower.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's second-term approval rating has plunged to a new low of 34% amid persistent inflation in food and gas prices, escalating tensions in the Iran conflict, and administration scandals including the Labor Secretary's resignation over misconduct allegations. Vice President JD Vance registers historically low popularity for a VP at this stage, undermining GOP frontrunner status in trader consensus despite leading Republican presidential nominee odds at 37.5%. Democrats hold double-digit leads on generic congressional ballots for the November 2026 midterms, fueling 60.5% implied probability for their 2028 presidential victory as the out-party benefits from an open-seat race after eight GOP years—historically favoring challengers in about 80% of cases since 1952. Midterm outcomes could solidify or shift this momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has surged to the top of recent California gubernatorial primary polls, leading or tying Republican Steve Hilton at 23-24% in surveys like Gudelunas Strategies and Impact Research from late April to early May, driving his 48.1% trader consensus as general election winner amid California's Democratic lean. Billionaire Tom Steyer trails closely at 36.5% implied probability, bolstered by fresh endorsements from Sierra Club and progressive groups like Our Revolution, plus self-funding advantages, as ballots arrive for the June 2 top-two primary. Eric Swalwell's April exit due to allegations consolidated Democratic support behind frontrunners, while Republicans like Hilton (6.5%) and Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco (2.9%) vie for a general election berth in the chaotic, wide-open field succeeding term-limited Gov. Gavin Newsom. A recent NBCLA debate sharpened contrasts on economy, housing, and climate ahead of the primary.

markets.question: Hurricanes vs. Flyers: O/U 5.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for May 9 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Hurricanes and Flyers combine to score 6 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 6, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes lead the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0 in their Eastern Conference Second Round Stanley Cup playoff series after a dominant 4-1 Game 3 win on May 7 at Wells Fargo Center, marking Carolina's seventh straight playoff victory and showcasing Frederik Andersen's strong goaltending alongside contributions from Jordan Staal and Andrei Svechnikov. Philadelphia faces mounting injury woes, with Noah Cates ruled out for the remainder of the series due to a lower-body injury, Owen Tippett sidelined again, and Christian Dvorak questionable but likely playing hurt, prompting a call-up of prospect Jett Luchanko. Game 4 returns to Philly on May 9, where home-ice desperation could fuel a Flyers response, but Carolina's depth, seven-game streak, and healthier roster define the current trader consensus amid the Flyers' depth chart strains.

eventMetadata.context_description: Retired U.S. Army Major General Bert Mizusawa leads trader consensus at 44.5% implied probability to win Virginia's Republican U.S. Senate primary on August 4, driven by a fresh Public Sentiment Institute poll (May 1-5) showing him edging Kim Farington 23.5%-22.3% among likely voters, with David Williams at 10.1%. Mizusawa's 36-year military career, West Point pedigree, and prior statewide runs boost his appeal to conservative primary voters seeking a battle-tested challenger to incumbent Sen. Mark Warner. Farington trails in market pricing despite poll closeness, while Williams lags; low odds for figures like Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears reflect doubts on their entry. No major endorsements or shifts in the past week, keeping the field competitive ahead of summer campaigning.

markets.question: Will Qatar vs. Switzerland end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 13, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Qatar vs. Switzerland
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Saturday, June 13, 2026 between Qatar and Switzerland.

eventMetadata.context_description: Switzerland enters this FIFA World Cup 2026 Group B opener as the clear trader favorite at 74% implied probability, driven by their superior FIFA ranking, depth from top European leagues, and consistent major tournament experience compared to Qatar's reliance on Asian Cup form and domestic-based talent. Recent closed-door sessions highlighted Switzerland polishing set-piece dominance and testing squad rotation amid Group B-wide injury concerns, while Qatar injected youth into compact defensive drills to spark competition for places, with no major withdrawals or fitness scares reported in the past week. The neutral Levi's Stadium venue in Santa Clara tempers any home advantage, but Switzerland's stylistic edge—quick transitions and aerial strength—over Qatar's counterattacking setup, plus a distant 1-0 friendly win for Qatar in 2018, solidifies the wide consensus gap.

markets.question: Will the United Arab Emirates send warships through the Strait of Hormuz by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a national government, its military, or a broad consensus of credible reporting confirms that the listed country's warships transited through the Strait of Hormuz between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A "warship transit" is defined as a military vessel passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Military cargo or support vessels will be considered “warships”; however, commercial or civilian vessels will not qualify.

For the purposes of this market, only transits through the Strait of Hormuz will be considered, defined as passage through the narrowest portion of the waterway between Iran and Oman. Operations solely in the Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, or Arabian Sea without passage through this narrowest section will not qualify.

Official confirmation by a national government or its military that its vessels transited through the Strait of Hormuz during the specified timeframe will resolve this market immediately. An overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming that such a transit occurred during the specified timeframe will also suffice.

Qualifying confirmations include statements such as official announcements that a country has deployed naval vessels to transit or escort shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

Confirmations referring only to naval presence in the broader region, including the Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, or Arabian Sea, without confirmed transit through the Strait itself, as well as aerial operations, cyber operations, or actions by proxies or third parties, will not alone qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information by the respective national governments or their militaries; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 100% implied probability for United States warships transiting the Strait of Hormuz by May 31, driven by recent U.S. Navy destroyer passages—including USS Truxtun, Mason, and Rafael Peralta—under Project Freedom amid Iran's blockade since late February, which has slashed daily ship traffic to under 15 vessels from a normal 100. Elevated Brent crude at $102 per barrel reflects supply risks through this chokepoint for one-fifth of global oil, while war-risk premiums have quadrupled to 1% of vessel values, surging tanker costs and stoking inflation pressures. UK and France outcomes linger at 13%, pending coalition decisions; key catalysts include U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks and potential allied escorts before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Strasbourg Alsace hold an advantageous position in the Ligue 1 table around 8th with 46 points from 30 matches, fueled by a dramatic 3-2 injury-time comeback victory at Lorient on April 26 that highlights their resilience amid a mixed run including losses to Rennes and Nice. Angers SCO, mired in 13th on 34 points from 31 games, struggle with poor recent form—drawing 1-1 versus Le Havre, losing 0-3 to PSG, and conceding heavily to Rennes and Lens—exacerbated by key injuries to Carlens Arcus (groin), Djibirin Harouna (ankle), and Yassin Belkhdim (arm). Strasbourg dominated earlier this season 5-0 at home, though Angers benefit from Stade Raymond Kopa home advantage and potential rest before matchday 33 on May 10. Trader consensus reflects Strasbourg's superior standing, momentum, and squad depth despite their own absences like Emanuel Emegha (muscle tear).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Shai Gilgeous-Alexander as a heavy 67% implied favorite for Western Conference Finals MVP, reflecting Oklahoma City Thunder's dominance as the No. 1 seed—sweeping Phoenix in the first round and surging to a 2-0 lead over the Lakers in conference semifinals despite SGA's uncharacteristically quiet 18-point Game 1. His bounce-back 22 points on efficient shooting in Game 2, combined with reigning MVP form averaging elite scoring and two-way impact, bolsters OKC's path to face San Antonio. Victor Wembanyama sits at 29.5% on the Spurs' tied 1-1 series versus Minnesota after his 19-point, 15-rebound Game 2 rout (133-95 win), leveraging his MVP finalist length against Chet Holmgren. Anthony Edwards' 7% reflects Timberwolves' Game 1 upset potential with his return.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Milan be 20°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Malpensa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Malpensa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/it/milan/LIMC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight clustering around 16–18°C (implied probabilities 20.5–21.5%) for Milan's highest temperature on May 10, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts showing daytime highs in the mid-teens Celsius amid an incoming Atlantic weather front. This system introduces persistent cloud cover, light drizzle, and cooler northerly winds, suppressing solar heating and capping peaks below seasonal norms of ~22°C. Model spreads of ±1–2°C stem from uncertainties in precipitation timing and boundary layer mixing, differentiating outcomes: earlier clearing favors 18–19°C, while prolonged overcast supports 16–17°C. Recent 00Z runs slightly lowered guidance from warmer weekend trends. Upcoming 12Z ECMWF updates will clarify exact thermal profiles before resolution.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

Ferrari trails closely at 90 points with strong Q3 threats from Leclerc and Hamilton, while midfield risers like Haas and Alpine eye upgrades; newcomers Audi Revolut and Cadillac lag but could surprise in low-downforce setup.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

Waddani leads party outcomes at 31% amid momentum from President Abdirahman Irro's 2024 presidential victory, positioning the party as frontrunner if polls proceed, while UCID trails at 5% and incumbent Kulmiye at 0.3% reflects post-loss challenges. Civil society urges timely polls, but institutional hurdles sustain delay probabilities ahead of the rescheduled March 2027 vote.

Nikita Kucherov sits close at 31.3%, buoyed by 130 points—second overall—and consistent dominance that vaulted Tampa Bay Lightning into playoff contention. Nathan MacKinnon's 15% share stems from Colorado Avalanche's Presidents' Trophy triumph (121 points, franchise record) and his Maurice 'Rocket' Richard win with 53 goals, plus elite 5-on-5 impact (+58 GF-GA). markets.question: T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women: China vs Hong Kong, China - Most Sixes China Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between China and Hong Kong, China scheduled for 2026-05-07 in T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to China will be considered correct if China is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Hong Kong, China.The outcome corresponding to Hong Kong, China will be considered correct if Hong Kong, China is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than China.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

The pitch offers pace and bounce but favors disciplined bowling over big shots, keeping totals modest and the most sixes outcome tightly poised between a low-volume draw and Hong Kong's marginal batting edge from recent form and head-to-head dominance.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Darth Vader" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Taiwan's Premier Cho Jung-tai continues serving amid ongoing legislative gridlock, with the opposition KMT and TPP controlling a majority in the Legislative Yuan, enabling potential no-confidence votes requiring a simple majority of 57 seats. Persistent budget disputes, including a 216-day review delay as of late April and an impeachment hearing against President Lai Ching-te—boycotted by the DPP—over Cho's prior refusal to countersign a fiscal bill, have fueled tensions but not triggered his removal. In the past week, Cho directed ministries to aid Taiwanese ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz amid Middle East escalations, while urging faster budget passage. No recent no-confidence motion has advanced, though upcoming arms procurement and appropriations votes could escalate risks to his tenure.

Carolyn Maloney and unions like DC 37. Assemblyman Micah Lasher holds 35% following Gov. Kathy Hochul's April 13 backing and Nadler's support, bolstering his West Side base, while Jack Schlossberg's 18% reflects Kennedy name recognition despite limited machine support and recent Working Families Party hesitation. eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Korea Meteorological Administration short-range forecasts and global model ensembles, including GFS and ECMWF, project Busan's highest temperature on May 9 around 23°C under mostly clear skies with light southerly winds and low precipitation risk, aligning with the market-implied odds favoring 23–25°C outcomes near 20% each. This trader consensus reflects recent observations—22°C peak on May 6 amid minimal cloud cover and 18–19°C on May 7 with lingering clouds—coupled with 2026's above-normal seasonal temperatures per KMA outlooks, boosting the 27°C or higher probability to 25.5% on warmer outlier runs showing stronger high-pressure subsidence. Key differentiators include afternoon cloud development potential and coastal sea breeze moderation versus inland heat advection; historical early-May averages hover at 20–22°C, but model spread underscores inherent short-range uncertainty ahead of twice-daily KMA updates resolving at Busan station observations.

This momentum, combined with recent polls showing a 13-point lead, reflects his appeal as a political newcomer emphasizing fiscal accountability amid voter frustration with rising costs. markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Nicolás Maduro at 62% to remain Venezuela's head of state by end-2026, despite his January US military capture and detention on drug charges, driven by persistent chavismo institutional control and UN recognition of his representatives. Acting President Delcy Rodríguez holds 24% implied probability after extending her interim role beyond the initial 90 days, bolstered by recent US sanctions relief, resumed direct flights on May 1, and diplomatic engagements with Colombia's Gustavo Petro on April 24 and Barbados PM Mia Mottley. Opposition leader María Corina Machado's 8% reflects her exile status and year-end return plans urging swift elections, amid no confirmed vote timeline or transition progress in the past month.

markets.question: Griekspoor vs. Blockx: Match O/U 22.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Tallon Griekspoor and Alexander Blockx in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 8:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 23. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Tallon Griekspoor vs Alexander Blockx
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Tallon Griekspoor and Alexander Blockx in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Tallon Griekspoor' if Tallon Griekspoor advances against Alexander Blockx.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Blockx' if Alexander Blockx advances against Tallon Griekspoor.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alexander Blockx's implied 67.5% probability reflects his explosive clay-court form during the spring swing, including a breakthrough semifinal run at the Mutua Madrid Open where the 21-year-old Belgian upset defending champion Casper Ruud and notched three straight Top 20 victories before falling to Alexander Zverev. Now ranked a career-high No. 36, Blockx backed his momentum with a gritty three-set comeback over wildcard Federico Cina in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia first round Thursday. Tallon Griekspoor, seeded No. 29 at No. 31 overall with an 8-10 record this year, enters via a bye after a modest Madrid showing—edging Damir Dzumhur before losing to Lorenzo Musetti—with no head-to-head history favoring the Dutchman's experience on the slower Roman clay.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's back-to-back Rolex Masters 1000 clay triumphs at Monte-Carlo—edging Carlos Alcaraz in the final—and a dominant 6-1, 6-2 Madrid Open rout of Alexander Zverev have propelled him to a 70.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite for the 2026 Roland Garros title, extending his streak to 28 straight Masters-level wins. Alcaraz's right wrist injury, forcing his withdrawal as defending champion, has cleared a major path, while Novak Djokovic (5.7%) continues limited clay preparation amid shoulder concerns. Zverev (7.2%) holds strong clay pedigree and recent Munich quarters but faltered badly against Sinner, positioning the field as competitive yet distant challengers ahead of the upcoming Rome draw.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader consensus favoring "No" at 75.5% stems from congressional gridlock on comprehensive AI safety legislation amid the Trump administration's light-touch regulatory framework.** The White House's March 2026 National Policy Framework emphasized preemption of state laws and narrow priorities like child protections and IP safeguards, stalling broader risk-based bills in a divided 119th Congress facing filibuster hurdles and midterm distractions. Recent bipartisan advances, such as the Senate Judiciary Committee's unanimous April 30 advancement of the GUARD Act mandating age verification for AI companions to minors, highlight focus on targeted measures rather than sweeping safety mandates. With seven months until 2027 resolution, no major bill has cleared committees, and historical patterns show low passage rates for tech regulation in lame-duck sessions. Upcoming NDAA provisions may add defense-related AI rules, but traders see slim odds for full enactment.

markets.question: Will another result occur for the next governing coalition of New Zealand?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

markets.question: Will London have less than 5mm of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in mm at Heathrow (London Airport) in May, 2026, according to the Met Office.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be the Met Office, specifically the figure for May 2026 under "rain mm" at the https://www.metoffice.gov.uk/pub/data/weather/uk/climate/stationdata/heathrowdata.txt link once the Provisional figure for the whole month of May 2026 is released.

If the relevant data is not available by July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after Provisional data is released for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku remains unsigned weeks after the 2026 NFL Draft, creating tightly bunched trader consensus atop the market as multiple cap-rich contenders vie for his services as a proven seam-stretching receiver and blocker. The former Browns star, who posted 81 catches for 937 yards and 6 TDs in 2025 before announcing in February he won't return to Cleveland amid their dead cap hit and new TE depth via draft and signings, recently visited the Los Angeles Chargers—bolstering their case alongside Justin Herbert—while drawing post-draft interest from the Ravens to pair with Mark Andrews. Cardinals, Broncos, Chiefs, and others lead at 46% implied probabilities due to young QBs like Kyler Murray, Bo Nix, and Patrick Mahomes needing reliable TE targets in thin free agency waters, with no dominant frontrunner amid ongoing negotiations and comparable scheme fits keeping the race deadlocked.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The U.S. labor market remains stable but shows signs of gradual cooling, with March 2026 nonfarm payrolls adding 178,000 jobs and the unemployment rate holding at 4.3 percent, per Bureau of Labor Statistics data. Recent JOLTS figures indicate job openings eased to 4.1 percent while hires rose, reflecting balanced demand amid rising layoffs totaling 128,000 in early 2026. Federal Reserve March Summary of Economic Projections median places Q4 2026 unemployment at 4.4 percent, aligning with economist consensus for a modest peak near 4.5 percent amid slowing growth. Traders eye today's April jobs report, expected at 62,000-67,000 additions, alongside June 16-17 FOMC meeting for policy signals on potential rate adjustments.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following certification of the April 16 special election results, Analilia Mejia's 60%-39% victory over Republican Joe Hathaway in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District—yielding a 21-point margin—has solidified trader consensus on the 20-25% outcome, reflecting the district's safely Democratic lean after Mikie Sherrill's resignation upon winning the governorship. Mejia overperformed Kamala Harris's 2024 presidential margin by roughly 12 points amid high special election turnout exceeding typical off-year levels, driven by strong Democratic mobilization in suburban Essex and Morris counties despite pockets of softer support in affluent areas like Livingston and Millburn. With canvassing complete and no close-race recount triggered, realistic challenges would require anomalous shifts from negligible remaining provisional or overseas ballots, though such adjustments rarely exceed 0.5 points in certified tallies.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be between 68-69°F on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices the 62-63°F outcome at a 100% implied probability for Seattle's highest temperature on May 7, 2026, backed by National Weather Service (NWS) hourly observations at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (KSEA), the market's resolution source, which recorded a daily peak of 63°F around 6:15 p.m. amid persistent marine stratus clouds, light northerly winds, and a cool air mass that suppressed daytime heating below the climatological May average of 64°F. This aligns with recent patterns following May 6's 57°F high under similar overcast conditions. Realistic challenges would require a rare post hoc revision to official KSEA data from sensor recalibration or overlooked peak, though NWS verification processes make this improbable; final daily climate summary expected soon.

eventMetadata.context_description: Blake Miguez leads trader consensus at 52.5% implied probability to win Louisiana's 5th Congressional District Republican primary, driven by President Trump's endorsement, dominant fundraising with nearly $4 million cash on hand as of mid-April, and a slight edge in the latest April 4-7 Rainey Center poll (23% to Michael Echols' 20% among 42% undecided likely voters). Echols, at 19.8%, benefits from stronger favorability ratings, $1.4 million cash on hand including self-funding, and attacks portraying Miguez as a non-resident "carpetbagger" outside district boundaries—though 45% of polled voters deem the Trump endorsement inconsequential. The May 16 closed primary, a new format for 2026 congressional races, remains closely contested in this open seat vacated by Rep. Julia Letlow, with early voting underway and no post-April polling. Minor PAC spending supports Echols while small opposition targets Miguez, underscoring fundraising's edge in consolidating support ahead of potential runoff.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Bigetron by Vitality and Team Liquid ID in the MPL Indonesia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 8 at 7:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Bigetron by Vitality and Team Liquid ID play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Bigetron by Vitality vs Team Liquid ID (BO3) - MPL Indonesia Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Bigetron by Vitality and Team Liquid ID in the MPL Indonesia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 8 at 7:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Bigetron by Vitality" if Bigetron by Vitality win the match against Team Liquid ID.

This market will resolve to "Team Liquid ID" if Team Liquid ID win the match against Bigetron by Vitality.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bigetron by Vitality sits third in MPL Indonesia Season 17 regular season standings at 6-4 (13-13 games), one match win ahead of fourth-place Team Liquid ID (6-5, 13-14), fueling trader focus on their Week 7 BO3 rematch scheduled for May 8. BTR's trader consensus edge stems from a 2-1 head-to-head win over TLID on April 18, extended by recent sweeps including 2-0 over RRQ Hoshi on May 2 and EVOS earlier in April, showcasing strong form amid patch 2.1.61 meta. TLID showed resilience with a 2-1 upset over NAVI on May 3 but faltered against top squads like ONIC and Dewa United. Both squads hold stable rosters—BTR featuring midlaner Moreno, TLID exp laner Aran—with top-6 playoff spots on the line in a congested race.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices stand at $2.67 per million British thermal units (MMBtu) as of May 4, 2026, with June futures (NGM26) settling near $2.79/MMBtu on May 7 amid abundant storage and shoulder-season demand. The EIA's May 7 weekly report revealed a 63 Bcf injection—below the 72 Bcf consensus—bolstering trader sentiment after production dipped monthly and LNG exports approached records at around 17 Bcf/d per EIA forecasts. Inventories at 2,142 Bcf through late April exceed five-year averages by roughly 100 Bcf, capping gains despite global demand. Key catalysts include weekly EIA storage updates (next May 15), cooling degree day forecasts signaling air conditioning needs, and LNG feedgas trends into summer.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 1360-1399 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

events.title: Wuxi: Adam Walton vs Yunchaokete Bu
events.description:

This market will resolve to 'Adam Walton' if Adam Walton advances against Yunchaokete Bu.

This market will resolve to 'Yunchaokete Bu' if Yunchaokete Bu advances against Adam Walton.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin edge for Adam Walton at 50.5% implied probability in this ATP Challenger Wuxi Round of 16 clash on outdoor hard courts, balancing his higher ranking (No. 103 vs. Bu's No. 152) and momentum from a Jiujiang Challenger final loss last week against Yunchaokete Bu's recent 6-4, 7-6(6) quarterfinal upset of Walton in Busan last month. Both advanced in straight sets Tuesday—Walton over Yu Hsiou Hsu 6-4, 6-4; Bu topping Keegan Smith—highlighting strong current form without reported injuries. Bu benefits from home-crowd support as a Chinese player, while Walton's superior YTD record (19-16 vs. Bu's 9-8) sustains the deadlock; late scratches, weather delays, or serve efficiency in tiebreaks could swing odds pre-match.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Slovenia's National Assembly remains without a government following the March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, which produced a hung parliament with Freedom Movement (GS) at 29 seats, SDS at 28, NSi-SLS-Fokus at 9, and smaller parties including Democrats (6), Resni.ca (5), Social Democrats (6), and The Left-Vesna (5). Incumbent PM Robert Golob's broad coalition bid collapsed on April 20 after rejections from center-right groups, prompting President Nataša Pirc Musar to decline nominating a prime minister-designate on April 25 due to insufficient 46-vote majorities. SDS leader Janez Janša now leads center-right talks, announcing coalition guidelines agreed on May 5 alongside a 49-vote parliamentary approval for reducing ministries from 19 to 14—signaling potential majority support. Final deal expected within days; failure risks minority government or snap election.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 85.5% implied probability against the US acquiring any part of Greenland in 2026, driven by repeated, firm rejections from Danish and Greenlandic leaders emphasizing the territory's sovereignty since President Trump's early-year revival of acquisition talks for Arctic national security against Russia and China. High-level diplomatic meetings in January, including with Vice President Vance and Secretary Rubio, yielded only vague frameworks for enhanced US military presence and cooperation, not sovereignty transfer, amid legal barriers under international law and NATO commitments. Symbolic US congressional bills like H.R.7012 advanced no further, public opinion polls showed low American support, and no formal purchase negotiations or consent emerged by May, solidifying barriers to any deal before year-end.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will fewer than 25 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between April 27-May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from April 27, 2026, through May 3, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.
